codeReview.summary: The feature adds the default-off tokenizer estimator flag and updates primary UsageStore upserts, but two blocking gaps remain: one ingestion upsert path still overwrites usageSource on equal-confidence collisions, and Hermes parsing still applies fallback estimates even when explicit token buckets exist.

issues.description: AtomicIngestionTransaction commit SQL still sets `usageSource = excluded.usageSource` on conflict, so equal-confidence collisions can overwrite source identity in this ingestion path. This violates the feature requirement to preserve source identity across ingestion paths.

Hermes JSONL ingestion always computes transcript fallback and uses `max(summary.inputTokens, estimated.input)` / `max(summary.outputTokens, estimated.output)`, so fallback logic can influence persisted tokens even when explicit usage buckets are present. This breaks the requirement that tokenizer fallback only apply to exact-missing cases.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Canonical `token_usage` upsert rules are duplicated across multiple files; the feature updated `UsageStore` but missed `AtomicIngestionTransaction` upsert semantics, causing invariant drift.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: AGENTS.md requires preserving `usageSource` identity; compare `AgentLens/Services/DataStore/UsageStore.swift` (new CASE logic) vs `AgentLens/Services/DataStore/ParserCheckpointStore.swift:305` (`usageSource = excluded.usageSource`).

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker handoff claims source identity is preserved for cross-source upserts, but transcript-reviewed output plus diff indicates not all upsert sites were audited.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff summary for session `c1550129-bb68-4afd-9dc2-0d1cbd7a5a1b` states source identity preservation was implemented; however `ParserCheckpointStore.swift:286-356` still contains pre-change conflict behavior.

summary: Review failed: implementation is partially correct but does not satisfy mission expectations end-to-end. Source identity preservation is incomplete across ingestion paths, and Hermes fallback behavior still allows estimator influence when explicit token usage exists.
